title: Unveiling the Amorphous Ice Layer during Premelting Using AFM Integrating Machine Learning
summary: Ice begins to change well before it melts. Even far below freezing, its surface forms a thin, liquidlike layer through premelting—a process relevant in cloud formation, cryopreservation, and planetary science. We provide a machine learning…
summary_zh: 冰在真正熔化前就会发生变化。即使远低于冰点，冰表面也会通过预熔形成一层薄薄的类液体层；这一过程与云的形成、低温保存和行星科学密切相关。我们提出了一种机器学习方法……

title: Atomic-resolution imaging reveals nucleus-free crystallization in two-dimensional amorphous ice on graphite
summary: Crystallization is a fundamental process in materials science and natural systems, underpinning phenomena ranging from mineral formation to the fabrication of functional materials. In three dimensional bulk systems, crystallization is commo…
summary_zh: 结晶是材料科学和自然系统中的基本过程，影响着从矿物形成到功能材料制备的多种现象。在三维体相体系中，结晶通常由经典成核理论描述；而在二维或界面环境中，这一过程更为复杂，也可能偏离经典图景……

title: Molecularly resolved mapping of heterogeneous ice nucleation and crystallization pathways using in-situ cryo-TEM
summary: The assembly of water molecules on foreign substrates, forming precipitation such as rain and snow, is a ubiquitous phenomenon that influences Earth’s ecosystems and environments. Despite broad and intense research interests, the scenario o…
summary_zh: 水分子在异质基底上组装并形成雨、雪等降水，是影响地球生态系统与环境的普遍现象。尽管相关研究广泛而深入，我们对异质冰成核与生长过程的认识仍不充分……

title: Probing structural superlubricity of two-dimensional water transport with atomic resolution
summary: Low-dimensional water transport can be drastically enhanced under atomic-scale confinement. However, its microscopic origin is still under debate. In this work, we revealed the atomic structure and transport of two-dimensional water islands…

title: Imaging surface structure and premelting of ice Ih with atomic resolution
summary: In the conventional view, the crystalline ice surface is simply considered as a truncated plane from the bulk without any reconstruction. However, it is commonly known that the atoms on solid surfaces tend to rearrange to minimize the surfa…

title: Nanoscale one-dimensional close packing of interfacial alkali ions driven by water-mediated attraction

title: Spin Nernst effect and spatiotemporal dynamic simulation of topological magnons in the antiferromagnet Cu3TeO6

title: Harvesting Energy from Changes in Relative Humidity Using Nanoscale Water Capillary Bridges
summary: We show that nanoscale water capillary bridges (WCB) formed between patchy surfaces can extract energy from the environment when subjected to changes in relative humidity (RH). Our results are based on molecular dynamics simulations combine…

title: Quantum criticality of a Z3 symmetric spin chain with long-range interactions
summary: Based on large-scale density matrix renormalization group techniques, we investigate the critical behaviors of quantum three-state Potts chains with long-range interactions. Using fidelity susceptibility as an indicator, we obtain a complet…

title: Fidelity susceptibility as a diagnostic of the commensurate-incommensurate transition: A revisit of the programmable Rydberg chain
summary: In recent years, programmable Rydberg-atom arrays have been widely used to simulate new quantum phases and phase transitions, generating great interest among theorists and experimentalists. Based on the large-scale density matrix renormaliz…

title: Machine learning aided atomic structure identification of interfacial ionic hydrates from AFM images
summary: Relevant to broad applied fields and natural processes, interfacial ionic hydrates have been widely studied by ultrahigh-resolution atomic force microscopy (AFM). However, the complex relationship between AFM signal and the investigated sys…

title: Visualizing Eigen/Zundel cations and their interconversion in monolayer water on metal surfaces
summary: Hydrated protons play a critical role in numerous physical and chemical processes. However, their atomic-level characterization remains experimentally challenging, especially for interfacial systems. Using qPlus-based atomic force microscop…

title: Glass polyamorphism in gallium: Two amorphous solid states and their transformation on the potential energy landscape
summary: Using the potential energy landscape (PEL) formalism and molecular dynamics simulations, we investigate a phase transformation between two amorphous solid states of gallium, namely, a low-density amorphous solid (LDA) and a high-density amo…

title: Nuclear quantum effects on the thermodynamic response functions of a polymorphic waterlike monatomic liquid
